result:
If I could alter the fabric of reality, I would not eliminate poverty, or war, or disease. Those are symptoms. I would address the cause.

I would instill in every human being a single, inescapable sense: a visceral, un-ignorable, physical echo of the joy and pain they cause in others.

It wouldn't be a thought or a moral lesson. It would be a sensation. If you broke someone's arm, you would feel a phantom, searing pain in your own. If you knowingly bankrupted a family, you would be haunted by the gnawing ache of their hunger and the cold dread of their homelessness. A lie told to a loved one would leave the taste of ashes in your mouth, a reflection of their heartbreak.

Conversely, an act of genuine kindness, a word of encouragement that lifts someone from despair, would send a warmth through your own chest. The joy of a child you made laugh, the relief of a stranger you helped—these would become as real and sought-after as a good meal or a deep sleep.

It wouldn't be a divine judgment or a system of karma. It would be a fundamental law of physics, like gravity. An unbreakable feedback loop woven into the nervous system of our species.

Free will would remain. You could still choose to hurt someone, but you would have to be willing to bear a shadow of that suffering yourself. Greed would become a form of self-mutilation. Cruelty would be a masochistic act. War would be a drawn-out, collective suicide.

It wouldn't make everyone "good." It would simply make the consequences of our actions personal and undeniable. Compassion would no longer be a virtue to be learned, but an instinct for survival. The pursuit of personal happiness would be inextricably linked to the well-being of the person standing next to you.

It wouldn't

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
